Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation."},"item_number":{"type":["number","string"],"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). 2026-03-23T22:09:50.4701371Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-23T22:07:14.159282076Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"safeoutputs","payload":{"id":2,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"tools":[{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. 2026-03-23T22:09:50.5013119Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-23T22:08:23.958990409Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":10,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":4098883239,\"number\":57231,\"state\":\"open\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding] UpdateAnalyzer/UpdateAnalyzerAsync docs should clarify which properties are updatable\",\"body\":\"## Description\\n\\nThe `UpdateAnalyzer` and `UpdateAnalyzerAsync` methods (and their protocol overloads) currently have XML doc summaries that say only \\\"Update analyzer properties\\\" without specifying **which** properties can actually be updated.\\n\\nThe underlying REST API uses `PATCH` with `application/merge-patch+json`, and **only `description` and `tags`** are accepted by the service. All other properties (`models`, `fieldSchema`, `config`, `baseAnalyzerId`, `dynamicFieldSchema`, `processingLocation`, `knowledgeSources`) are create-only — they are silently ignored by the server even though it returns HTTP 200.\\n\\nThis has caused customer confusion — see #57215.\\n\\n## Requested Improvements\\n\\n1. **API doc (`\\u003csummary\\u003e` / `\\u003cremarks\\u003e`)**: Add a `\\u003cremarks\\u003e` section to `UpdateAnalyzer`/`UpdateAnalyzerAsync` listing the updatable properties and noting that other properties require `CreateAnalyzerAsync` with `allowReplace: true`.\\n\\n2. **Sample doc (Sample08_UpdateAnalyzer.md)**: Add a note in the \\\"About updating analyzers\\\" section explaining that only `description` and `tags` are updatable via this method, and reference `CreateAnalyzerAsync` with `allowReplace: true` for changing `models`, `fieldSchema`, etc.\\n\\n## Updatable Properties\\n\\n| Property | Updatable via `UpdateAnalyzer` (PATCH)? |\\n|---|---|\\n| `description` | **Yes** |\\n| `tags` | **Yes** |\\n| `models` | No — silently ignored |\\n| `fieldSchema` | No — silently ignored |\\n| `config` | No — silently ignored |\\n| `baseAnalyzerId` | No — silently ignored |\\n| `dynamicFieldSchema` | No — silently ignored |\\n| `processingLocation` | No — silently ignored |\\n| `knowledgeSources` | No — silently ignored |\\n\\n## Workaround\\n\\nTo change create-only properties on an existing analyzer, use `CreateAnalyzerAsync` with `allowReplace: true`. This calls `PUT` (full resource replacement) instead of `PATCH`.\\n\\n## Related\\n\\n- #57215 — customer report of `UpdateAnalyzerAsync` not updating `models`\\n\",\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"chienyuanchang\",\"id\":186005646,\"node_id\":\"U_kgDOCxY4jg\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/186005646?v=4\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/chienyuanchang\",\"gravatar_id\":\"\",\"type\":\"User\",\"site_admin\":false,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/chienyuanchang\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/chienyuanchang/events{/privacy}\",\"following_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/chienyuanchang/following{/other_user}\",\"followers_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/chienyuanchang/followers\",\"gists_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/chienyuanchang/gists{/gist_id}\",\"organizations_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/chienyuanchang/orgs\",\"received_events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/chienyuanchang/received_events\",\"repos_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/chienyuanchang/repos\",\"starred_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/chienyuanchang/starred{/owner}{/repo}\",\"subscriptions_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/chienyuanchang/subscriptions\"},\"labels\":[{\"id\":1178447860,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Client\",\"name\":\"Client\",\"color\":\"ffeb77\",\"description\":\"This issue is related to a non-management package\",\"default\":false,\"node_id\":\"MDU6TGFiZWwxMTc4NDQ3ODYw\"},{\"id\":9758131317,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Cognitive%20-%20Content%20Understanding\",\"name\":\"Cognitive -
